# Silver And Data Vault

Operate silver-layer and Data Vault workflows, referential checks, and point-in-time access.

## kiri dv

Data Vault tooling - silver-quality checks...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri dv [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

None.

### Subcommands

- [`kiri dv apply`](#kiri-dv-apply) - Graph-driven idempotent DV silver apply...

- [`kiri dv install`](#kiri-dv-install) - Install / refresh DataVault4dbt for a...
- [`kiri dv migrate-zones`](#kiri-dv-migrate-zones) - Phase-3 migration helper for (raw...
- [`kiri dv quality`](#kiri-dv-quality) - Silver-quality checks for Data Vault...
- [`kiri dv test`](#kiri-dv-test) - Run DV invariants against CSV fixtures...

### kiri dv apply

Graph-driven idempotent DV silver apply...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri dv apply [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--json` | Emit the run manifest as JSON on stdout instead of a table. | no | False |  |
| `--backfill` | Declared backfill mode: drop + recreate satellites and PIT from --from-batch forward. Hubs + links are preserved (insert-only). Requires --from-batch and --reason; prints the plan and requires --yes before any destructive apply. | no | False |  |
| `--from-batch` | Batch id to rebuild from (inclusive). Required with --backfill. | no |  |  |
| `--reason` | Audit justification for the backfill. Required with --backfill. | no |  |  |
| `--yes` | Confirm a destructive backfill. Without it, --backfill prints the plan and exits 0 (dry-run). | no | False |  |

### kiri dv conformance

Diff the native + DataVault4dbt backends...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri dv conformance [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--fixture-dir` | Directory of per-table CSV fixtures (same convention as `kiri dv test`). Each <node>.csv mirrors the bronze source shape; the harness runs it through both backends and diffs the result. Missing directory aborts with exit-2. | no | conformance |  |
| `--json` | Emit the conformance report as JSON on stdout instead of a table. | no | False |  |

### kiri dv graph

Render a bounded Mermaid view of the...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri dv graph [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--view` | Which bounded view to render: 'overview' (domain/zone roll-up - the default), 'domain:<d>', 'zone:<z>' (raw\|business\|pit), 'node:<n>' (BFS ball around a node; pair with --depth), or 'path:<a>..<b>' (shortest path). A mature vault is never rendered as one flat graph - pick a scope. | no | overview |  |
| `--depth` | BFS radius for --view node:<n> (ignored otherwise). | no | 1 |  |
| `--max-nodes` | Hard node budget. Over budget collapses lowest-priority nodes into counted summaries (default 50). | no | 50 |  |
| `--format` | 'mermaid' (default - deterministic, GitHub/mkdocs-ready) or 'json' (overview → bounded domain rollup; scoped views → the view's DataVaultGraphPlan sub-plan = the BFF wire shape). | no | mermaid |  |

### kiri dv install

Install / refresh DataVault4dbt for a...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri dv install [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--target`, `-t` | Active dbt target name. Must match the DataVault4dbt compatibility matrix (snowflake, bigquery, postgres, redshift, synapse, fabric, databricks, oracle, exasol). Defaults to the project's default target. | no |  |  |

#### kiri dv quality run

Run the silver-quality catalogue against...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri dv quality run [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--target`, `-t` | Named profile from kiri.yml | no |  |  |
| `--gate` | Comma-separated check IDs that block sign-off when failing (e.g. DV-001,DV-002). | no |  |  |
| `--exclude` | Comma-separated check IDs to skip entirely. | no |  |  |
| `--max-samples` | Cap on violation rows per check (default 100). | no | 100 |  |
| `--output`, `-o` | Base path for the report. With --format both this writes <output>.json and <output>.md; with --format json/md it writes the single requested file at the literal path. | no |  |  |
| `--format` | Output format: json \| md \| both (default both). | no | both |  |
| `--ai-summary`, `--no-ai-summary` | Add a Kiri AI paragraph at the top of the report. Audit-logged via the AI gateway. Default off. | no | False |  |

### kiri dv test

Run DV invariants against CSV fixtures...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri dv test [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--fixture-dir` | Directory containing per-table CSV fixtures. Each <table>.csv must match a DV contract's name + the contract's silver-build column shape. Missing files produce a warning per uncovered contract; missing directory aborts with exit-2. | yes |  |  |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--output` | Output format: 'rich' (default) or 'json'. | no | rich |  |

## kiri fk

Foreign-key referential-integrity gate...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri fk [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

None.

### Subcommands

- [`kiri fk gate`](#kiri-fk-gate) - Probe declared foreign keys against the...

### kiri fk gate

Probe declared foreign keys against the...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri fk gate [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--target`, `-t` | Named profile from kiri.yml | no |  |  |
| `--exclude` | Comma-separated check IDs to skip. Only FK-001 and FK-002 may be excluded; FK-003 (dangling parent) and FK-004 (self-cycle) are structural hard FAILs and cannot be suppressed. | no |  |  |
| `--max-samples` | Cap on sample rows returned per probe (default 100). | no | 100 |  |
| `--max-cycle-depth` | Bounded depth (hops) for the FK-004 self-cycle probe (default 16). A cycle longer than this is NOT detected - the bound is surfaced in the finding, never a silent cap. | no | 16 |  |

### kiri silver apply

Materialise the project (silver included),...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri silver apply [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |

### kiri silver conformance

Run the technique-appropriate silver...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri silver conformance [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--fixture-dir` | Fixture directory for the Data Vault cross-backend conformance run. Required when silver.technique=data_vault; ignored for flat / kimball_dimensional (their conformance needs no fixtures). | no |  |  |
| `--out` | Where to write the evidence JSONL. Defaults to <project>/.kirimana/quality_engine/evidence.jsonl so `kiri compliance report` picks it up. | no |  |  |
| `--report`, `--no-report` | Also run the per-run silver-quality report against the live target - RI checks plus the technique's conformance findings - and print it. Mirrors `kiri dv quality run` for flat / Kimball. Not applicable to data_vault (use `kiri dv quality run`). | no | False |  |
| `--target`, `-t` | Named profile from kiri.yml for the --report live RI checks. | no |  |  |
| `--report-format` | --report output format: json \| md \| both (default both). | no | both |  |

### Examples

```bash
kiri dv quality run``, and prints it in ``--report-format``.
```

## kiri timetravel

Query an SCD2 silver table as it looked at...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri timetravel [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--target`, `-t` | Named profile to use (dev\|test\|prod). Falls back to KIRIMANA_TARGET or the project's default_target. | no |  |  |
| `--source` | Source name | yes |  |  |
| `--table` | Table name | yes |  |  |
| `--at` | ISO timestamp to query at, e.g. '2026-03-01' or '2026-03-01T12:00:00Z'. | yes |  |  |
| `--limit` | Max rows to print. Set 0 for no limit. | no | 50 |  |
| `--schema` | Schema where silver lives. Default 'silver' (dbt default). | no | silver |  |
